top_signals.title: Aluminum Posts Fourth Weekly Decline on Mideast Supply Return
top_signals.summary: Aluminum capped a fourth weekly decline, the longest losing streak since April 2025, as a renewed selloff in tech stocks added to pressure from the dollar’s recent rally and returning Middle East supplies.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Equinor Pulls Out of Japan’s Offshore Wind Market
top_signals.summary: Equinor is ending its offshore wind business activities in Japan and will close its Tokyo office by the end of the year as the Norwegian energy major is realigning its non-oil and gas strategy to focus on power markets. “This decision reflects a reassessment of Equinor’s strategic direction, with a strengthened focus on integrated power markets,” the Norwegian company said on Friday. While Equinor is ending its Japanese presence, Japan will remain an important country for Equinor, which will continue to invest in its longstanding…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Japan quake strikes near Mount Fuji, but experts see low eruption chance
